{{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Kami}} |Festival={{jalink|April 15|Q2497}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōda City|Q384796}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki shrine}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{jalink|2018|Q25291}}, the shrine’s {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{jalink|Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{jalink|San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{jalink|Ōda City|Q384796}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} ** {{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Kami}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{jalink|April 15|Q2497}} * Divine virtues ** {{jalink|Protection from misfortune}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{jalink|Safe childbirth}} prayers ** {{jalink|Business}} prosperity ** {{jalink|Bountiful harvest}} ** {{jalink|Academic success}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}, {{jalink|1928|Q19715}}. The {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}} date, which had previously been {{jalink|October 22|Q2957}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}, {{jalink|1928|Q19715}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{jalink|April 9|Q2504}}, {{jalink|2018|Q25291}} – The {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{jalink|Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048}} (see {{jalink|Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|below}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{jalink|April 9|Q2504}}, {{jalink|2018|Q25291}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{jalink|Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{jalink|AIST}} {{jalink|Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{jalink|Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{jalink|Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{jalink|Asahi Shimbun Digital}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{jalink|Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{jalink|Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{jalink|Lake Hane|Q55523943}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{jalink|Holocene|Q25445}} sedimentary layers near the {{jalink|Sea of Japan|Q27092}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karitajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:苅田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892535}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Karita Shrine |Image={{Image requested|cat=Ōda City}} |Location={{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Ōda City|Q384796}} 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities=Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto
Karita-hime-no-Mikoto
{{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Kami}} |Festival={{jalink|April 15|Q2497}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōda City|Q384796}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki shrine}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{jalink|2018|Q25291}}, the shrine’s {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{jalink|Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{jalink|San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{jalink|Ōda City|Q384796}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} ** {{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Kami}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{jalink|April 15|Q2497}} * Divine virtues ** {{jalink|Protection from misfortune}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{jalink|Safe childbirth}} prayers ** {{jalink|Business}} prosperity ** {{jalink|Bountiful harvest}} ** {{jalink|Academic success}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}, {{jalink|1928|Q19715}}. The {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}} date, which had previously been {{jalink|October 22|Q2957}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}, {{jalink|1928|Q19715}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{jalink|April 9|Q2504}}, {{jalink|2018|Q25291}} – The {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{jalink|Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048}} (see {{jalink|Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|below}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{jalink|April 9|Q2504}}, {{jalink|2018|Q25291}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{jalink|Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{jalink|AIST}} {{jalink|Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{jalink|Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{jalink|Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{jalink|Asahi Shimbun Digital}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{jalink|Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{jalink|Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{jalink|Lake Hane|Q55523943}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{jalink|Holocene|Q25445}} sedimentary layers near the {{jalink|Sea of Japan|Q27092}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karitajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill||lt=AIST|UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill||lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karitajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

* {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички Shimane|tg|Ода|tl|Ōda, Shimane|tr|Ōda|tt|Ода (шәһәр)|uk|Ода (Сімане)|vi|Ōda, Shimane|war|Ōda, Shimane|zh_classical|大田市|zh_min_nan|Ôda-chhī|zh_yue|大田市|zh|大田市|WD=Q384796}} == coordinate location (P625) 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:苅田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892535}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Karita Shrine |Image={{Image requested|cat=Ōda City}} |Location={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}} 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities=Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto
Karita-hime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} |Festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|UNKNOWN}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karitajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama|de|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|eo|Ukanomitama|fr|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|mni|ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|ru|Ука-но митама|simple|Ukanomitama|sv|Ukanomitama|th|อูกาโนมิตามะ|zh|宇迦之御魂神|WD=Q3080728}} * {{ill|Q478504|lt=Q478504|WD=Q478504}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * かりたじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 694-0051 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Kamutano Shrine|lt=Kamutano Shrine|WD=Q135041107}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県大田市久手町波根西', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:苅田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892535}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Karita Shrine |Image={{Image requested|cat=Ōda City}} |Location={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}} 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities=Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto
Karita-hime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karitajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karitajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

* {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa Shimane|tg|Ода|tl|Ōda, Shimane|tr|Ōda|tt|Ода (шәһәр)|uk|Ода (Сімане)|vi|Ōda, Shimane|war|Ōda, Shimane|zh_classical|大田市|zh_min_nan|Ôda-chhī|zh_yue|大田市|zh|大田市|WD=Q384796}} == coordinate location (P625) 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:苅田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892535}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Karita Shrine |Image={{Image requested|cat=Ōda City}} |Location={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}} 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities=Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto
Karita-hime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karitajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karitajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karitajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]
