|Name=Karita Shrine |Image={{Image requested|cat=Ōda City}} |Location={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}} 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities=Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto
Karita-hime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} |Festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110|1=San-in Chuo Shimpo}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below|1=Karita Shrine}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703|1=Geological Survey of Japan}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943|1=Lake Hane}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445|1=Holocene}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karitajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

Karita-hime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} |Festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110|1=San-in Chuo Shimpo}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below|1=Karita Shrine}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703|1=Geological Survey of Japan}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943|1=Lake Hane}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445|1=Holocene}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karitajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943|1=Lake Hane}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445|1=Holocene}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

Shrine|WD=Q135041107|1=Kamutano Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県大田市久手町波根西', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:苅田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134892535}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Karita Shrine |Image={{Image requested|cat=Ōda City}} |Location={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}} 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities=Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto
Karita-hime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} |Festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110|1=San-in Chuo Shimpo}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below|1=Karita Shrine}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703|1=Geological Survey of Japan}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943|1=Lake Hane}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445|1=Holocene}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Oda, Shimane]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

|Name=Karita Shrine |Image={{Image requested|cat=Ōda City}} |Location={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}} 1942-2 Karita-hime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} |Festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110|1=San-in Chuo Shimpo}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below|1=Karita Shrine}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703|1=Geological Survey of Japan}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943|1=Lake Hane}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445|1=Holocene}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Oda, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ota]] [[Category:pages]]

The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below|1=Karita Shrine}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703|1=Geological Survey of Japan}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943|1=Lake Hane}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445|1=Holocene}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Oda, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ota]] [[Category:pages]]

|Name=Karita Shrine |Image={{Image requested|cat=Ōda City}} |Location={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}} 1942-2 Karita-hime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} |Festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110|1=San-in Chuo Shimpo}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below|1=Karita Shrine}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703|1=Geological Survey of Japan}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943|1=Lake Hane}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445|1=Holocene}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Oda, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ota]]

|Name=Karita Shrine |Image={{Image requested|cat=Ōda City}} |Location={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}} 1942-2 Karita-hime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} |Festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|WD=Q11471110|1=San-in Chuo Shimpo}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|WD=Q19715|1=1928}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|WD=Q134892535|lt=below|1=Karita Shrine}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|WD=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|WD=Q25291|1=2018}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|WD=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|WD=Q11424703|1=Geological Survey of Japan}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|WD=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|WD=Q55523943|1=Lake Hane}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|WD=Q25445|1=Holocene}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Oda, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ota]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} |Festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Karita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{Reading|'''Karita Shrine'''|かりたじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|qid=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}{{Cite web |title=Karita Shrine |url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html |website=www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|qid=Q25291|1=2018}}, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|qid=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}{{Cite web |title=Meisō: Preparedness for earthquakes |url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/380827 |website= |date=2023-05-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=San-in Chūō Shinpo|Q11471110|qid=Q11471110|1=San-in Chuo Shimpo}} Digital}}{{Cite web |title=Seismic intensity 5+ in Shimane Prefecture; damage to buildings and roads in succession |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/category/society/390019 |publisher=Nittele NEWS NNN |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja-JP |last=Nippon Television}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.ckcnet.co.jp/ckcwp/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/20180409_shimane_eq01.pdf Preliminary report on damage from the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}. === Overview === * Enshrined location: {{ill|lt=Ōda City|Q384796|qid=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, 1942-2 Hanenishi, Kute-chō * Principal enshrined deities ** Karita-hiko-no-Mikoto ** Karita-hime-no-Mikoto ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} * Chief priest: Hiroshi Tsubokura * Annual festival date: {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}} * Divine virtues ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Protection from misfortune|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Exorcism of spiritual possession ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Safe childbirth|1=UNKNOWN}} prayers ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Business|1=UNKNOWN}} prosperity ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Academic success|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === The shrine was originally enshrined in Kamitani, Kute-chō, but was relocated to its current site on {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|qid=Q19715|1=1928}}. The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} date, which had previously been {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|qid=Q2957|1=October 22}}, was also changed to April 15 to coincide with the date of relocation. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}}, {{ill|lt=1928|Q19715|qid=Q19715|1=1928}} – Relocated from the original enshrinement site (Kamitani, Kute-chō) to the present site (Hanenishi, Kute-chō). * {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|qid=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|qid=Q25291|1=2018}} – The {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|qid=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}} (see {{ill|lt=Karita Shrine#Torii collapse due to the earthquake|Q134892535|qid=Q134892535|lt=below|1=Karita Shrine}}). === Torii collapse due to the earthquake === In the early morning of {{ill|lt=April 9|Q2504|qid=Q2504|1=April 9}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|qid=Q25291|1=2018}}, an inland, shallow-focus earthquake of magnitude 6.1 occurred in western Shimane Prefecture (the {{ill|lt=Western Shimane Earthquake|Q52105048|qid=Q52105048|1=2018 Shimane earthquake}}). A maximum seismic intensity of 5+ was observed in Ōda City near the epicenter, where damage was concentrated{{PDFlink|[https://ibaraki-saito.sakura.ne.jp/image/20180409simaneotajisinhigai.pdf On the earthquake that occurred in the vicinity of Ōda City, western Shimane Prefecture, in the early morning of April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.oda.lg.jp/files/original/20190918150632495a6b32ed8.pdf The Western Shimane Earthquake with an epicenter in eastern Ōda City]}}{{Cite web |title=Related information on the earthquake that occurred in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018 (Heisei 30) |url=https://www.gsj.jp/hazards/earthquake/shimane2018/index.html |website=www.gsj.jp |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=AIST|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Geological Survey of Japan|Q11424703|qid=Q11424703|1=Geological Survey of Japan}} / Geological Survey of Japan, AIST}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in Shimane; five people injured; building damage |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20180409/k00/00e/040/152000c |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|qid=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake with intensity 5+ in Shimane; four injured in Ōda City |url=https://www.risktaisaku.com/articles/-/5672 |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher=Risk Taisaku.com |date=2018/04/09}}. As a result of this earthquake, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} gate within the grounds of Karita Shrine collapsed into pieces, blocking the roadway{{Cite web |title=[Shimane Earthquake] “A roaring sound rang out and everything in the house fell over”—cracks in roads, torii collapse; water supply difficulties in Ōda City |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180409-Q6VDKOQUQRKTJD3YCNXFQTUE5U/ |website= |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|qid=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{Cite web |title=Shimane Prefecture western area hit by intensity 5+ earthquake; no tsunami concern |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180409102417/https://www.asahi.com/articles/photo/AS20180409000151.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2018-04-09 |access-date=2024-12-31 |publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asahi Shimbun Digital|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{Cite web |title=Earthquake: intensity 5+ in western Shimane Prefecture; no tsunami concern |url=https://mainichi.jp/graphs/20180409/hpj/00m/040/002000g/20180409hpj00m040022000q |website= |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|qid=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} |date=2018/4/9}}{{Cite web |title=[Photo Gallery][Shimane Earthquake] Five injured, water outages at 108 households; Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshihide Suga: “Remain vigilant” |url=https://www.sankei.com/gallery/20180409-W4LP5V454FLQVEXZ5HHBGCNWCI/ |website= |date=2018/4/9 |access-date=2024-12-31 |language=ja |publisher={{ill|lt=Sankei Shimbun|Q1197261|qid=Q1197261|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}{{PDFlink|[https://peshimane.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/2018-01_%E5%B3%B6%E6%A0%B9%E7%9C%8C%E8%A5%BF%E9%83%A8%E5%9C%B0%E9%9C%87%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%82%8B%EF%BC%88%E5%9D%82%E7%94%B0%E8%81%96%E4%BA%8C%E3%80%81%E6%9C%88%E6%A3%AE%E5%8B%9D%E5%8D%9A%E3%80%81%E7%95%91%E5%92%8C%E5%AE%8F%E3%80%81%E4%BA%95%E4%B8%8A%E7%9C%9F%EF%BC%89.pdf Looking back on the Western Shimane Earthquake]}}{{PDFlink|[https://anzen.eng.tottori-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/sites/6/2018/05/67e369bffc4f354f3e7862199ea2c25d.pdf Investigation report on the earthquake in western Shimane Prefecture on April 9, 2018]}}{{PDFlink|[https://isss.jp.net/isss-site/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/2018_shimane_seibu_eq_mori-20180419presented.pdf Preliminary briefing on on-site damage investigation of the Western Shimane Earthquake on April 9, 2018]}}. Kute-chō and Hane-chō in Ōda City—including the Hanenishi district where Karita Shrine is located—were among the areas that suffered particularly severe damage in this earthquake. In addition to the presence of {{ill|lt=Lake Hane|Q55523943|qid=Q55523943|1=Lake Hane}} and the Kute reclaimed land, liquefaction damage is also thought to have occurred due to the distribution of {{ill|lt=Holocene|Q25445|qid=Q25445|1=Holocene}} sedimentary layers near the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|qid=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}} in this region{{Cite web |title=Geology and earthquakes of the Ōda region—Learning from the 2018 Western Shimane (Ōda) Earthquake— |url=https://sanbesan.web.fc2.com/kouza_shiryou/kouza_ohda_180515.html |website=sanbesan.web.fc2.com |access-date=2024-12-31 |date=May 15, 2018}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/oda/353e7f8659f584c2a2ebf6fdc5d14643f9e941a3.html Karita Shrine] – Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Pref-stub|pref=Shimane Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karita Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Oda, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ota]]
